title: Your brain on nature: A scoping review of the neuroscience of nature exposure.

assets.abstract: The relationship between natural environments and human cognition has gathered increasing attention across disciplines, including neuroscience, environmental psychology, and public health. An expanding body of empirical evidence supports the notion that exposure to nature consistently promotes psychological and physiological well-being. However, our understanding of the neural mechanisms underlying these benefits remains limited. This scoping review synthesizes experimental findings from neuroim

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
